title: BEM-928 dialogue report
objective:
Create file governance/reports/BEM928_DIALOGUE_ANALYTICS_REPORT.md with content # BEM-928 DIALOGUE ANALYTICS REPORT
Status: ANALYTICAL_DIALOGUE_REPORT_READY
Date: 2026-05-31
Purpose: structured analytical report based on operator request to summarize the dialogues by themes, problems, decisions and implications for the final working-system protocol.
Scope:
- Main themes discussed across yesterday/today dialogues.
- Problems encountered.
- Decisions accepted.
- What must be transferred into final protocol/roadmap.
- Claude audit requirements as release blockers.
Report structure:
1. Executive summary.
2. Problem register.
3. Chronological/thematic analysis of 34 dialogue blocks.
4. Decision register.
5. What must be included in final protocol.
6. Claude audit mapping.
7. Recommendations before final protocol assembly.
8. Mistakes that must not be repeated.
Key captured decisions:
- Objects GD/DIR/WRK have object passports and rules, not prompts.
- Prompts belong only to LLM roles/providers/operational handlers.
- Internal contour algorithm: Curator -> Analyst -> Auditor -> Executor -> Auditor -> Curator with revision through Analyst.
- Vertical communication: curator-to-curator.
- Horizontal communication: Auditor A -> Analyst B or Analyst B request to Auditor A.
- Workspace belongs to internal contour, not element.
- No local element rules by default.
- Provider model: GPT Analyst, Claude Auditor, Claude Executor with GPT-Codex/Codex fallback for Claude roles.
- Telegram is an operator front, not system center.
- Release PASS requires non-null CI/Git SHA and Claude-verifiable evidence.
Local operator artifacts:
- BEM928_dialogue_analytics_report_ru.docx
- BEM928_dialogue_analytics_report_ru.zip
QA: DOCX rendered to 8 pages; no edge clipping detected.
No issue comments.
